constructs: implicit evaluation, explicit evaluation, information type, temporal learning, symbolic reasoning
claim: Implicit and explicit evaluations of the same object form and change independently because they depend on different information modalities and processing systems.

phenomenon: early false-belief attribution in action prediction
constructs: mental state attribution, false belief, action anticipation, theory of mind, competence vs performance
claim: Infants at 25 months attribute false beliefs to others and use those attributions to predict behavior, challenging the conceptual-deficit account of false-belief failures.

constructs: attention, temporal selection, efficacy, latency, precision, dissociation
claim: The attentional blink reflects not a unitary bottleneck but three independent mechanisms affecting selection amplitude, timing, and spread with distinct recovery dynamics.

phenomenon: robust ensemble representation outside focal attention
constructs: attention focus, local features, summary statistics, ensemble pooling, representational hierarchy
claim: Unattended items maintain degraded local representations but support accurate statistical summaries through pooling, indicating multiple representational levels.

constructs: probabilistic representation, repeated estimation, error independence, internal distribution, anchoring
claim: Human knowledge is sampled from internal probability distributions rather than deterministically accessed, evidenced by mutual error reduction when averaging successive self-estimates.

constructs: temperature, social proximity, embodied cognition, language abstraction, perceptual focus, metaphorical grounding
claim: Temperature is bidirectionally linked to social distance through embodied metaphor, shaping language, perception, and proximity judgments.
